title: Design Leadership in Action: The Case of Cory Barlog
summary: This paper examines Cory Barlog, Creative Director of Sony's Santa Monica Studio, as a case study in contemporary design leadership, analysing how his direction of God of War (2018) represents a landmark example of visionary, transformational, and reflexive leadership in AAA game development. The paper explores how Barlog's personal experiences as a new father directly shaped the emotional core of the game, particularly the Kratos-Atreus relationship, while his commitment to iterative precision, developmental transparency, and servant-leadership principles fostered a psychologically safe and creatively empowered studio culture. His leadership is analysed through transformational and visionary frameworks, highlighting how he balanced creative autonomy with operational discipline, facilitated interdisciplinary collaboration through Nordic cultural research trips, and helped redefine AAA narrative benchmarks through innovations like the one-shot camera technique. The second part presents a fictional executive recruitment brief for a Creative Director of Narrative Innovation at Santa Monica Studio, modelled on the competencies Barlog embodied.
